patch.value: pm health takes 8s and reports ok:false due to blocking telemetry flush to unreachable endpoint

pm health=7950ms and ok:false. Root cause: telemetry check performs a synchronous flush to pm.unbrained.dev which is unreachable from this host (DNS/connection timeout), so the flush blocks and the warn flips ok to false. Fix: bound the flush with a short timeout, treat unreachable telemetry as a warn that does not flip ok:false, avoid blocking health on network.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Root cause was NOT the telemetry probe (checkTelemetry defaults false). Two real causes: (a) ok:false because health computed ok = (warnings.length===0), so the advisory telemetry_queue_pending warning flipped overall health to not-ok; (b) the ~6.8s wall time was the history_drift check re-reading + JSON-parsing + hash-chain-verifying all 858 streams (17MB) every run. Fixes: (1) isAdvisoryHealthWarning() — telemetry_* warnings are surfaced but never flip ok (telemetry is opt-out, non-critical); (2) drift-scan.ts now caches per-stream verification keyed by history file mtime/size (runtime/history-drift-cache.json, atomic write) — unchanged streams skip re-read/re-verify, item-side changes still caught by recomputing the document hash. Measured: pm health 8453ms -> ~1300ms (6.5x), ok:true. New drift-scan.spec.ts gives drift-scan.ts 100% coverage (69/69 stmts, 36/36 branches); two health-command tests updated to assert advisory (ok stays true).

patch.value: pm health 8.4s->1.3s (drift verification cache) and ok:true (telemetry warnings now advisory, never flip overall health). Full 100% coverage on new drift cache.
